sections.paragraph:
- Original: οὐ

- Transliteration: Ou

- Phonetic: oo

- Definition:

1. no, not; in direct questions expecting an affirmative answer

- Origin: a primary word, the absolute negative [cf G3361] adverb

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech:

- Strong's: Also οὐκ ouk ook used before a vowel and οὐχ ouch ookh before an aspirate. A primary word; the absolutely negative (compare G3361) adverb; no or not: - + long nay neither never no (X man) none [can-] not + nothing + special un ([-worthy]) when + without + yet but. See also G3364 G3372.
